mission.title: cilium: Multiple consecutive retries of updating taints and conditions for a node failed
mission.description: Multiple consecutive retries of updating taints and conditions for a node failed. Users encounter: ""Node.core \"XXXXX\" not".

steps.title: Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"Node.core \"XXXXX\" not found"`

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

### Version

equal or higher than v1.17.1 and lower than v1.18.0

### What happened?

Hi there,

Since the upgrade of v1.17.1, we have a ton of such warn logs:

```
level=warn msg="Multiple consecutive

steps.title: Apply the fix for Multiple consecutive retries of updating taints and…
steps.description:
The Cilium Operator logs are filled with attempts to retry updating taints/conditions on nodes even if we can't get the node from the local store due to node deletion. Skip retry in this case.
```yaml
cni:
  chainingMode: aws-cni
  exclusive: false
enableIPv4Masquerade: false
routingMode: native
endpointRoutes:
  enabled: true
dnsProxy:
  endpointMaxIpPerHostname: 500
```

steps.title: Confirm Multiple consecutive retries of updating taints… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"Node.core \"XXXXX\" not found"` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: The Cilium Operator logs are filled with attempts to retry updating taints/conditions on nodes even if we can't get the node from the local store due to node deletion. Skip retry in this case.
codeSnippets:
cni:
  chainingMode: aws-cni
  exclusive: false
enableIPv4Masquerade: false
routingMode: native
endpointRoutes:
  enabled: true
dnsProxy:
  endpointMaxIpPerHostname: 500

* Notes: 
  * I had to remove the node with `kubectl delete`, without draining first. 
  * Restarting the ds (`kubectl rollout restart ds/cilium -n kube-system`) did not fix the issue.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
